title: The Claw of the Great Horned Owl
description: Discover the powerful grip of the great horned owl's talons and how they relate to spring nesting behaviors.
main_content: The great horned owl (Bubo virginianus) is one of North America's most formidable raptors. Its claws, or talons, are specialized for gripping and killing prey. Each foot has four toes: three forward-facing and one reversible toe that can rotate backward for better grip. The talons are curved, sharp, and up to 2 inches long. In spring, as they nest, these talons are crucial for carrying prey to owlets and defending the nest. The grip strength of a great horned owl can exceed 500 psi, allowing it to crush the spines of small mammals instantly. Watch for these owls in wooded areas at dusk.
key_takeaways: Great horned owl talons have a grip strength of over 500 psi.
Their reversible toe allows versatile grasping.
Spring nesting season makes talons vital for feeding young and defense.
